name: Chronology Social Impact Layer
summary: Historical-to-social translation layer showing how large-scale events alter class behavior, institutions, professions, mobility, faith practice, and present-day NPC psychology across regions.

social_effects: families treat continuity as fragile and invest more heavily in memory-keeping, oaths, and estate control
storm windows, dangerous seas, and magical instability reward institutions that can promise continuity
folk religion becomes materially protective, not merely devotional
profession_effects: wardens, archivists, navigators, priests, engineers, and ritual specialists gain social weight
sailors and caravan brokers become interpreters of risk, not just transport workers

major_transitions.transition: Rise of the Union over Mal-mediated trade
social_effects: merchant legitimacy shifts from hereditary house trust toward enforceable network leverage
old landed elites become more image-sensitive and procedurally defensive
urban witnesses grow cautious because commercial retaliation can arrive before formal justice
profession_effects: brokers, sanctions clerks, contract readers, and warehouse captains gain influence
road houses and market towns become narrative battlegrounds over legitimacy

social_effects: beauty and civic refinement become evidence of successful risk management
mutation fear drives soft exclusion, paperwork screening, and exile policy rationalized as communal protection
profession_effects: dome stewards, mutation screeners, cantrip educators, and containment researchers acquire prestige and quiet coercive authority

social_effects: duty language replaces sentiment in public justification of hardship
class and species roles are narrated as structural necessity rather than naked hierarchy
profession_effects: bridge crews, descent captains, furnace schedulers, food allocators, and maintenance leaders become civic keystones

young_adults.principalities: Learned that paperwork, sponsorship, and visible affiliation determine safety as much as personal virtue.
young_adults.sheresh: Normalize magical convenience but also inherit contamination anxiety and beauty-as-discipline norms.
young_adults.soreheim: Experience duty through production metrics, ration access, and tower role assignment.
young_adults.psanan: Measure worth through daring, gifts, and what one can survive or bring home.
young_adults.nomdara: See trust as portable and negotiable, tied to repeated exchange rather than fixed soil.

midlife_power_holders.principalities: Remember Mal's displacement and therefore read trade autonomy as political threat.
midlife_power_holders.sheresh: Balance pride in magical culture with fear that one failed containment decision becomes communal shame.
midlife_power_holders.soreheim: Treat system continuity as morally superior to personal discomfort.
midlife_power_holders.psanan: Carry vow logic seriously and see domestic authority as strategic civilization, not sentiment.
midlife_power_holders.nomdara: Price peace through supply security and reputation memory.

elder_memory_bearers.principalities: Preserve house grievance maps, route memories, and quiet knowledge of how law is selectively enforced.
elder_memory_bearers.sheresh: Remember harsher mutation eras and can become either containment hardliners or mercy advocates.
elder_memory_bearers.soreheim: Remember tower failures and therefore tolerate coercion in the name of continuity.
elder_memory_bearers.psanan: Carry blood-vow precedent, wartime marriage memory, and ancestral door custom without apology.
elder_memory_bearers.nomdara: Maintain route compacts and remember which polities break promises only when frightened.

campaign_use: When generating an NPC reaction, locate their cohort before selecting demeanor.
When adjudicating rumors, ask which cohort would remember older house or route arrangements.
When presenting reform or alliance offers, model age-specific risk tolerance and shame sensitivity.
